response.justification: The candidate is a strong, communicative full-stack engineer but does not match the core requirements of this role. The mandatory ask is 5–10 years of hands-on C++ on Windows plus SQL Server/Oracle and capital-markets/fixed-income domain knowledge. His only substantial C/C++ work is roughly six months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 plus an academic thesis; his recent decade is in DLang, TypeScript/Node.js, and web/GIS systems. There is no fixed-income or SQL Server/Oracle exposure, and the lone finance-adjacent role (RIDE Capital) was Node/GraphQL, not C++. Communication skills are clearly excellent, but they cannot offset the missing primary technical and domain criteria.
strengths: Excellent verbal/written communication, evidenced by talks, workshops, and courses
Broad, deep engineering background with strong TDD/CI-CD and craftsmanship practices
Some finance-sector exposure via RIDE Capital (investment platform)
concerns: Only ~6 months of professional C++; nowhere near the required 5–10 years on Windows
No SQL Server/Oracle and no fixed income / capital markets domain experience
Recent stack (DLang, TypeScript, Node.js) is far from the role's C++/Windows focus

key_factors.factor: No fixed income / capital markets domain
